summary: Competitors are aggressively enhancing their converged offerings through high-value content bundles and AI-driven user experience improvements, most notably Sky’s integration of HBO Max and EE’s launch of conversational TV search. Infrastructure competition is intensifying as Vodafone introduces software-led speed enhancements to challenge traditional broadband performance leads, while BT prioritizes large-scale digital voice upgrades. These developments highlight a market shift toward value-added services and automated technology to justify premium pricing during high-inflation periods.

signals.headline: HBO Max included for Sky and NOW customers from 26 March, bringing more world-class entertainment in one subscription
signals.impact: This significantly strengthens Sky’s entertainment leadership and creates a competitive content gap for Virgin Media’s TV proposition.

signals.headline: EE TV Transforms the TV Experience by Becoming the UK’s First Pay-TV Service to Introduce Conversational, AI-Powered Search
signals.impact: The use of generative AI for discovery raises the technological benchmark for TV interfaces, potentially making Virgin’s current platform feel dated.

signals.headline: Speed Boost offers Vodafone customers up to 2x faster speeds
signals.impact: This directly attacks Virgin Media's primary differentiator of speed leadership by offering performance upgrades to existing Vodafone broadband customers.
